title: ML System Design
description: Production ML system design patterns — recommendation, fraud detection, search ranking, ad prediction, content moderation, personalized feeds, deployment strategies, and monitoring.
details: ML system design at FAANG scale requires multi-stage architectures: candidate generation (retrieval 1000s of items), ranking (deep model on cross features), and re-ranking (diversity, business rules). Key tradeoffs: latency vs accuracy, compute cost vs model complexity. Every system needs monitoring for data drift, concept drift, and prediction distribution shifts. Deployment follows shadow → canary → full rollout with automatic rollback.

sections.title: Design a Recommendation System — Two-Tower DNN Architecture
sections.description:
[Intuition] The two-tower model separates user and item representation into independent embedding spaces. The query tower processes user features, the item tower processes item features, and their similarity is measured by dot product. This lets you precompute item embeddings offline and use fast approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) search at serving time.

[Practical Workflow] Retrieve 1000 candidates via ANN → rank with deep model → re-rank top 20 for diversity → serve.
sections.codeBlock:
import tensorflow as tf

# Two-tower model for recommendation
# Query tower: user features -> 64-dim embedding
# Item tower: item features -> 64-dim embedding
# Trained with sampled softmax loss

class TwoTowerModel(tf.keras.Model):
    def __init__(self, num_items, embedding_dim=64):
        super().__init__()
        # Query tower: user features
        self.query_tower = tf.keras.Sequential([
            tf.keras.layers.Dense(256, activation='relu'),
            tf.keras.layers.Dense(128, activation='relu'),
            tf.keras.layers.Dense(embedding_dim, activation='relu'),
        ])
        # Item tower: item features
        self.item_tower = tf.keras.Sequential([
            tf.keras.layers.Dense(256, activation='relu'),
            tf.keras.layers.Dense(128, activation='relu'),
            tf.keras.layers.Dense(embedding_dim, activation='relu'),
        ])
        # Item embeddings for candidate retrieval
        self.item_embeddings = tf.keras.layers.Embedding(num_items, embedding_dim)

    def call(self, user_features, item_features, training=False):
        # Encode user and item
        query = self.query_tower(user_features)
        item = self.item_tower(item_features)
        # Dot product score
        return tf.reduce_sum(query * item, axis=-1, keepdims=True)

    def get_scores(self, user_features, item_ids):
        query = self.query_tower(user_features)
        item_emb = self.item_embeddings(item_ids)
        return tf.reduce_sum(query * item_emb, axis=-1)

sections.title: Design a Fraud Detection System
sections.description:
[Concrete Numbers] Fraud is extremely imbalanced: 99.9% legitimate, 0.1% fraudulent. A model that predicts "legitimate" for every transaction gets 99.9% accuracy but catches zero fraud. We optimize precision@k and recall at specific thresholds.

[Practical Workflow] Transaction arrives → extract features (amount, velocity, device fingerprint, geolocation) → score with XGBoost + GNN in <100ms → flag if score > threshold → human review for borderline cases.
sections.codeBlock:
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np

# Fraud detection with XGBoost + class imbalance handling
# Features: transaction amount, velocity, device score, geolocation distance, user age

model = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    n_estimators=500,
    max_depth=6,
    learning_rate=0.05,
    scale_pos_weight=100,  # Neg/pos ratio for imbalance: 99.9/0.1 ≈ 1000, tune down
    eval_metric='auc',
    use_label_encoder=False
)

# Alternative: focal loss via custom objective
def focal_loss(alpha=0.25, gamma=2.0):
    def loss(y_true, y_pred):
        pt = y_pred * y_true + (1 - y_pred) * (1 - y_true)
        focal = -alpha * (1 - pt) ** gamma * np.log(pt + 1e-8)
        return focal
    return loss

# model.fit(X_train, y_train, eval_set=[(X_val, y_val)])

sections.title: Design a Search Ranking System
sections.description:
[Intuition] Search ranking is fundamentally different from recommendation — the user has an explicit intent (the query), so relevance to the query is paramount. We need both lexical matching (BM25 for exact term matches) and semantic matching (DPR for conceptual understanding).

[Practical Workflow] Query → spelling correction & expansion → retrieve 1000 via BM25 + DPR hybrid → rank with LambdaRank → re-rank top 10 for diversity → serve.
sections.codeBlock:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import TfidfVectorizer

# Hybrid retrieval: BM25 (term) + DPR (dense) with reciprocal rank fusion

def reciprocal_rank_fusion(bm25_scores, dpr_scores, k=60):
    """Combine sparse and dense retrieval scores."""
    # Convert scores to ranks, then fuse
    bm25_ranks = np.argsort(np.argsort(-bm25_scores))
    dpr_ranks = np.argsort(np.argsort(-dpr_scores))
    fused = np.sum([1.0 / (k + bm25_ranks), 1.0 / (k + dpr_ranks)], axis=0)
    return np.argsort(-fused)

# LambdaRank objective: pairwise loss that optimizes NDCG directly
# Conceptually: gradient = -|ΔNDCG| * (1 / (1 + exp(s_i - s_j)))
# where ΔNDCG is the change in NDCG if we swap items i and j

sections.title: Design an Ad Prediction (CTR) System
sections.description: [Concrete Numbers] Meta's ad system: 10B+ features, 100TB+ training data daily, sub-50ms inference. Features are extremely sparse — most users don't click most ads. FTRL-Proximal handles this by keeping per-feature learning rates.

sections.title: Design a Content Moderation System
sections.description:
[Intuition] Content moderation uses a tiered approach: cheap heuristics catch the majority of violations (fast & high recall), deep models handle edge cases, and human reviewers handle borderline decisions. This minimizes cost while maintaining high coverage.

[Concrete Numbers] YouTube's moderation pipeline processes 500+ hours of video per minute. T1 heuristics catch 60% of spam with zero model cost. Only <5% of content reaches human review.

sections.title: Design a Personalized News Feed
sections.description:
[Intuition] A news feed must balance relevance, diversity, and freshness. Without diversity (MMR), the feed fills with similar content. Without freshness penalties, users see the same posts repeatedly. Ads must be inserted at a fixed ratio.

[Practical Workflow] Generate 500+ candidates → neural scorer → filter seen/NSFW → diversify via MMR → blend with ads → serve ranked + diversified feed.
sections.codeBlock:
import numpy as np

def maximal_marginal_relevance(scores, embeddings, lambda_val=0.5, top_k=10):
    """Diversify results: MMR = λ·score - (1-λ)·max_sim(selected, candidate)"""
    selected = []
    candidates = list(range(len(scores)))

    for _ in range(top_k):
        best_idx = -1
        best_val = -np.inf
        for i in candidates:
            sim_to_selected = 0.0
            if selected:
                similarities = embeddings[i] @ embeddings[selected].T
                sim_to_selected = np.max(similarities)
            mmr_val = lambda_val * scores[i] - (1 - lambda_val) * sim_to_selected
            if mmr_val > best_val:
                best_val = mmr_val
                best_idx = i
        selected.append(best_idx)
        candidates.remove(best_idx)
    return selected

# Fatigue penalty: decay factor for recently seen content
# score = base_score * exp(-num_impressions / decay_rate)

sections.title: Data Drift vs Concept Drift Detection
sections.description:
[Intuition] Data drift: features look different but the relationship is the same (e.g., seasonal effects — winter has higher coat sales, but the P(buy|weather) is unchanged). Concept drift: the relationship changed (e.g., COVID-era buying patterns vs pre-COVID — the same features now predict different outcomes).

[Practical Workflow] Monitor PSI (Population Stability Index) on features weekly. If PSI > 0.2, investigate the feature. Monitor model confidence distribution daily. If confidence drops across the board, flag concept drift. Retrain on recent data for gradual drift; rebuild pipeline for sudden drift.
sections.codeBlock:
import numpy as np
from scipy.stats import ks_2samp

def population_stability_index(expected, actual, bins=10):
    """PSI measures how much a feature distribution has shifted."""
    # Discretize into bins
    expected_perc = np.histogram(expected, bins=bins, range=(0, 1))[0] / len(expected)
    actual_perc = np.histogram(actual, bins=bins, range=(0, 1))[0] / len(actual)
    # Avoid division by zero
    expected_perc = np.clip(expected_perc, 1e-4, 1.0)
    actual_perc = np.clip(actual_perc, 1e-4, 1.0)
    # PSI = sum((actual - expected) * ln(actual / expected))
    psi = np.sum((actual_perc - expected_perc) * np.log(actual_perc / expected_perc))
    return psi
    # Interpret: PSI < 0.1 = no shift, 0.1-0.25 = moderate, > 0.25 = significant

def detect_concept_drift(model, X_recent, y_recent, threshold=0.05):
    """Detect concept drift by comparing recent vs expected error rate."""
    expected_error = 0.1  # Baseline from validation
    recent_preds = model.predict(X_recent)
    recent_error = np.mean(recent_preds != y_recent)
    # KS test: are recent errors from the same distribution as expected?
    stat, p_value = ks_2samp(recent_error, expected_error)
    return p_value < threshold  # Significant drift if True

sections.title: Shadow & Canary Deployment
sections.description: [Practical Workflow] Shadow for 1 week (compare predictions, no user impact) → canary 1% for 1 day → canary 10% for 1 day → canary 50% for 1 day → 100% rollout. If at any point metrics regress (latency spikes 50ms+, accuracy drops 1%+, error rate exceeds threshold), rollback immediately via feature flag. Automate rollback triggers based on metric thresholds.
sections.codeBlock:
# Deployment stages with automated rollback conditions
DEPLOYMENT_STAGES = [
    {'name': 'shadow',   'traffic': 0,   'duration_days': 7,  'rollback_if': None},
    {'name': 'canary_1', 'traffic': 0.01, 'duration_days': 1, 'rollback_if': {'latency_p99': 1.5, 'error_rate': 2.0}},
    {'name': 'canary_10','traffic': 0.10, 'duration_days': 1, 'rollback_if': {'latency_p99': 1.5, 'error_rate': 2.0}},
    {'name': 'canary_50','traffic': 0.50, 'duration_days': 1, 'rollback_if': {'latency_p99': 1.3, 'error_rate': 1.5}},
    {'name': 'prod',     'traffic': 1.0,  'duration_days': -1, 'rollback_if': None},
]
# Rollback threshold: if metric exceeds baseline by X factor, revert
# E.g., latency_p99 > 1.5× baseline for 5 consecutive minutes → auto-rollback

sections.title: Model Retraining Strategy
sections.description:
Retraining frequency depends on the system's sensitivity to data staleness:

SystemRetrain FrequencyMethodStaleness Tolerance
Ads CTRHourlyFTRL online (incremental)Minutes
SearchDailyFull retrain + online smoothingHours
RecommendationWeeklyFull retrain (neural)Days
Content ModerationMonthlyFull retrain + active learningWeeks

[Practical Workflow] Automation: training pipeline triggers on (1) data freshness — new data available, (2) schedule — cron-based, (3) drift detection — PSI > threshold. For incremental models (FTRL), update per-coordinate weights online. For neural nets, full retrain on recent window + warm-start from previous weights.
sections.codeBlock:
import datetime

def retraining_trigger(last_trained, data_freshness, drift_score):
    """Automate retraining decisions."""
    hours_since_train = (datetime.datetime.now() - last_trained).total_seconds() / 3600
    max_age_hours = {
        'ads_ctr': 1,          # Retrain hourly
        'search': 24,          # Retrain daily
        'recsys': 168,         # Retrain weekly
        'moderation': 720,     # Retrain monthly
    }
    if hours_since_train > max_age_hours['ads_ctr']:
        return 'schedule_based'
    if drift_score > 0.25:
        return 'drift_detected'
    if data_freshness > 100_000:  # 100K new samples
        return 'data_availability'
    return 'no_action'

sections.title: Monitoring ML Systems
sections.description:
[Practical Workflow] Track these metrics on dashboards (Grafana + Prometheus / WhyLabs / Arize / Evidently):

CategoryWhat to TrackAlert If
PredictionsMean, std, quantiles, % > 0.9Mean shifts > 2σ from historical
FeaturesMin, max, mean, std, null count, PSIPSI > 0.25, null rate > 5%
Latencyp50, p95, p99, throughput (req/s)p99 > 200ms for real-time
Data FreshnessLast training time, last feature updateTraining > 2× expected interval
Label LeakageIs ground truth available in time?Feature uses future information

[Interview Angle] "Your model's accuracy dropped 5% in the last hour. What do you do?" — Check prediction distribution first (did all predictions shift?), then feature drift (PSI), then data freshness (did the training pipeline fail?), then concept drift (is the ground truth distribution changing?). Never jump to retraining without diagnosis.

sections.title: FAANG Q&A — ML System Design
sections.description:
[Interview Angle] ML System Design questions test depth of production experience. Interviewers want to hear trade-off reasoning, not just architecture.

  1. Google L5: "Design a recommendation system for YouTube." — Start with data: 500M users, billions of videos, implicit feedback (watch time). Two-tower with cross-attention ranking. HNSW for candidate retrieval. Multi-stage: retrieve 1000 → rank 100 → re-rank 20. Key metric: watch time, not clicks.
  2. Meta E5: "How would you detect and respond to data drift in a production model?" — PSI on features, KS test, monitor prediction distribution. Response: root cause first (is it a pipeline bug or real drift?), then retrain on recent data, if sudden drift, rollback to previous model version.
  3. Amazon L5: "Design a fraud detection system for PayPal-scale transactions." — Imbalance: 99.9% legit. Features: amount, velocity, device, geo. XGBoost + GNN for relational. Real-time <100ms. Precision@k priority (don't block legit users). Human-in-the-loop for borderline cases.
  4. Google L4: "How do you safely roll out a new model version?" — Shadow (compare logs) → canary 1% → 10% → 50% → 100%. Rollback via feature flag. Monitor: latency, error rate, prediction distribution. Auto-rollback if any metric exceeds 2σ.
  5. Meta L5: "Your model's accuracy dropped 5% in the last hour. What do you do?" — Triage: check if it's a data pipeline issue (feature nulls, schema change), drift, or serving infrastructure. Check prediction distribution — if all predictions are 0.8 now vs 0.5 before, that's drift. Check PSI on top features. Rollback if needed, then root cause.
